$comment: Template for skill eval cases (EDD — Evaluation-Driven Development). Copy this structure into evals/<skill-name>.eval.json. Minimum 3 cases per skill: at least 2 positive (this skill SHOULD trigger) and 1 negative (this skill should NOT trigger, even though the input superficially resembles a trigger). Write these cases before or alongside SKILL.md changes, not after — that's the 'D' in EDD. Checked by catalog/check_eval_triggers.py via simple keyword-overlap (no LLM call) — see CONTRIBUTING.md.
skill: <skill-name, matching the SKILL.md frontmatter name field exactly>

cases.input: <a realistic user message that SHOULD trigger this skill>
cases.notes: <what this case verifies — which real-world phrasing or use case>

cases.input: <a second realistic user message, phrased differently, that SHOULD trigger this skill>
<a realistic user message that should NOT trigger this skill, despite superficial similarity>
cases.notes: <the false-positive routing failure this guards against — usually tied to the skill's 'Do NOT use for...' description clause>
